track.title: Diaspora Investing Kenya
track.description: A starter route for Kenyans abroad comparing Kenya goals, currency, verification, and trusted execution boundaries.

course.title: Diaspora Investing Kenya: Core route
course.description: Plan Kenya-linked money decisions from abroad without handing over private credentials or rushing.

lessons.title: Separate home goals from investment routes
lessons.summary: Diaspora users should separate family support, emergency reserves, property goals, and long-term investing.
lessons.introduction: Kenyans abroad often manage several money roles at once: family support, school fees, construction, land searches, emergency support, and personal investing. A route becomes safer when each goal has its own timeline, currency, documents, and person responsible.
sections: Family support should not be mixed with money needed for formal investment decisions.
A Kenya goal paid in KES has currency timing risk if income or savings are in another currency.
A trusted relative can help gather information, but they should not need bank, broker, or M-Pesa credentials.

scenario.title: Three goals in one account
scenario.text: A diaspora user has KES support, a land viewing plan, and MMF curiosity. They split each goal into separate notes before choosing any route.

lessons.mistake: Sending all money to one person or account without separating purpose, timeline, and evidence.
lessons.action: Create three buckets: family support, near-term Kenya obligation, and learning/investment route.
lessons.takeaway: Diaspora planning starts by separating goals before comparing products.
lessons.source_note: Source note: This is education only; cross-border tax and legal questions need qualified review.

lessons.title: Currency, transfer, and documentation risk
lessons.summary: Transfers, exchange rates, documentation, and proof of source can affect cross-border money routes.
lessons.introduction: A diaspora route is not only about where money ends up. The route includes currency conversion, transfer fees, recipient controls, proof of funds, tax questions, and documentation needed if the money is later used for property, securities, or business.
sections: FX spreads and transfer charges reduce the amount arriving in Kenya.
Tax treatment depends on the activity and jurisdiction; PesaRoute does not provide tax advice.
Documentation should show why money was sent, who received it, and what decision it supports.

scenario.title: KES land deposit from abroad
scenario.text: A user plans to send money for a land deposit. They first ask what documents prove the parcel, seller, agreement, and payment purpose.

lessons.mistake: Only comparing transfer speed while ignoring proof, fees, tax questions, and recipient authority.
lessons.action: For one transfer, record sending currency, receiving currency, fee, FX rate, recipient role, and document purpose.
lessons.takeaway: The transfer route is part of the investment decision.
lessons.source_note: Source note: Verify tax obligations and legal documents with official sources and qualified professionals.

lessons.title: Using professionals without over-sharing
lessons.summary: Professional review should be scoped; users do not need to expose everything by default.
lessons.introduction: Diaspora users may need licensed or qualified professionals for capital-market, SACCO, land, legal, or tax questions. Privacy-first review means the user shares the minimum useful context and keeps credentials private.
sections: A professional can review a question without needing M-Pesa PINs, bank passwords, broker credentials, or account control.
Data sharing should be explicit, scoped, revocable, and time-limited.
The user can share ranges instead of exact amounts when exact values are not necessary.

scenario.title: Professional review from abroad
scenario.text: A user asks a licensed professional to review an MMF route and shares only amount range, goal, timeline, and question.

lessons.mistake: Giving a professional or agent login credentials instead of sharing scoped information.
lessons.action: Write the question, the minimum context needed, and what must stay private.
lessons.takeaway: Professional help should reduce risk without creating new credential risk.
lessons.source_note: Source note: Check relevant registers and use written scopes for professional review.

lessons.title: Diaspora journal before sending money
lessons.summary: Use a journal note to capture route, recipient, evidence, and privacy choices before sending funds.
lessons.introduction: A diaspora journal note creates a private record before money moves. It should capture why money is being sent, who receives it, what documents support the decision, and what information the user refuses to share.
sections: Write the purpose in plain language: school fees, land review, MMF learning, SACCO question, or emergency support.
Record evidence received, such as official source links, documents, or professional review notes.
Use hidden or range amount mode if exact values are not needed for the learning step.

scenario.title: Before transfer
scenario.text: A user records: purpose is land document review, amount range hidden, recipient is lawyer, official search pending, no credentials shared.

lessons.mistake: Sending money first and reconstructing the decision later from chat messages.
lessons.action: Create a diaspora journal entry with purpose, recipient, evidence, unanswered questions, and privacy boundary.
lessons.takeaway: A written decision trail helps protect cross-border money decisions.
lessons.source_note: Source note: This is a planning note, not legal, tax, or investment advice.

quiz_questions.prompt: What should never be shared with a professional through PesaRoute?
What belongs in a diaspora transfer note?
options: Currency, fee, recipient role, and purpose
quiz_questions.correct_answer: Currency, fee, recipient role, and purpose

options: Different goals have different timelines and risks
quiz_questions.correct_answer: Different goals have different timelines and risks
quiz_questions.explanation: Family support, emergencies, and investing should not be mixed blindly.

flashcards.back: A money goal that will be paid or used in Kenya, often in KES.
flashcards.example: School fees, family support, land review, or SACCO deposits.

flashcards.back: A conversion cost between the reference exchange rate and the rate actually used.
flashcards.example: The KES received may be lower than a headline rate suggests.

flashcards.back: They explain purpose, recipient, and evidence behind the decision.
flashcards.example: Agreement, official search, or professional review note.

flashcards.back: Sharing only the information needed for a specific review.
flashcards.example: Share amount range and question, not account login.

flashcards.back: It lets the user keep exact values private when exact values are not needed.
flashcards.example: Record KES 100k-500k instead of a precise amount.

resources.title: Diaspora route checklist
resources.body: A checklist for Kenyans abroad before sending money for investment-related decisions: separate goals, document transfer route, verify recipient role, check official sources, and keep credentials private.
items: Separate support, emergency, property, and investment goals.
Record FX rate, fee, and recipient role.
Verify source or professional status where relevant.
Do not share PINs, passwords, broker credentials, or one-time codes.
